content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against `ca4d23414de261e5b7d9716c8c608569045ab18d`, read the changed implementation files end-to-end, and checked the `result` untracking change. One medium fix-now issue remains: checkpoint cleanup intentionally skips standard ignore sources outside repository `.gitignore`, so the bead's tracked-but-ignored cleanup is not fully implemented.

## Amendments

1. `src/adapters/worktree.rs:142-152` narrows tracked ignored-path detection to `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-per-directory=.gitignore`, and `build_checkpoint_tree` only removes the paths returned by that scan (`src/adapters/worktree.rs:269-274`). That means tracked files ignored via other standard Git ignore sources, notably `.git/info/exclude` and `core.excludesFile`, still survive into checkpoint commits. The new tests at `tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:437-533` explicitly lock in that narrower behavior. The bead requires the standard-ignore behavior (`--exclude-standard` or equivalent), so this leaves `rb: checkpoint` incomplete and still able to leak unrelated locally ignored tracked files into diffs.
   Rationale: This is the same correctness problem the bead is trying to solve, just for the rest of Git's standard ignore sources instead of only repository `.gitignore` files. A local reproduction shows `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard` reports these paths while the current `--exclude-per-directory=.gitignore` call does not.
